body.title: Wire classify() into /ticket skill — BuilderReport at filing time
body.result: cli.py classify/freshness; palace query wired to clan.memories; /ticket step 3.5; /sprint-ticket step 2.4; 8 tests
body.intention: I intend that every filed ticket has a BuilderReport section so DS reads relevant_files at sprint start without exploratory searching.
body.description:
Wire classify() call into /ticket skill so BuilderReport is written into the ticket description at filing time as a labeled section (consistent with existing convention — no DB migration needed). Sprint start calls freshness_check() only (fast path, no LLM). Skill-to-device call mechanism: subprocess call to devices/classifier CLI or MCP tool — resolve and name at sprint start.

Ticket description gains a section:
**Builder report:** {"relevant_files": [...], "confidence": 0.9, "stale": false, "ts": "..."}

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/skills/ticket (skill file), cc_queue.py (if ticket schema needs builder_report field), devices/classifier/ (CLI entry point)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; flat-file safe (classify call is best-effort — if device is down, ticket files without builder report, no gate)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: classify() called at filing, BuilderReport in ticket description, freshness_check() at sprint start. Out of scope: in_flight flags (T-classifier-inflight-flags), delta updates (T-annotator-delta-update), multi-project classification.
**Completion criteria:** T-builder-report-at-filing is CLOSED; new tickets filed via /ticket have **Builder report:** section in description; freshness_check() at sprint start returns BuilderReport without LLM call when files unchanged; classify() failure is non-fatal (ticket files successfully without report if device is down).
